pattern: Powers of each base grow fast, so only a small grid of exponent pairs stays under the bound; enumerate that grid and collect the distinct sums.
hints: Both x^i and y^j grow at least geometrically once the base exceeds 1, so very few exponents keep the sum under the bound.
Iterate i while x^i stays below the bound, and inside that, j while x^i + y^j stays within it; collect every sum into a set to drop duplicates.
Beware bases equal to 1: 1^i is always 1, so cap the exponent loop (e.g. break when the power exceeds the bound, treating base 1 specially) to avoid looping forever.

edge_inputs.description: two distinct bases under a small bound
both bases are 1, only sum 2 qualifies
bound below the minimum sum of 2 yields empty
bound exactly equal to the minimum sum

stress.description: random small bases with varied bounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    x = rng.randint(1, 6)
    y = rng.randint(1, 6)
    bound = rng.randint(0, 1000000)
    return [x, y, bound]
stress.note: small bases keep the exponent grid manageable while exercising duplicates
